t_ch: 爻光将军，您想要的诚意，是希望由我们捉拿此人，移交仙舟？
t_en: General Yao Guang, is the sincerity you seek for us to capture this man and deliver him into the hands of the Xianzhou?

t_ch: 我听闻「幻月游戏」开启在即，举世翘首期盼，您恐怕也难以从中抽身。如果这个灾星能悄无声息地「自行离去」，岂不是皆大欢喜？
t_en: I hear the Phantasmoon Games are about to begin, with the entire nation awaiting it with bated breath. I imagine you will also find it hard to extract yourself from the festivities. If this cursed man were to quietly "depart on his own" at this time... would that not be a perfect outcome for all?

t_ch: 我的理解：您想要在「二相乐园」之上行事自由的权利。
t_en: This is my understanding: you wish for the right to act freely within Planarcadia.

t_ch: 在一同对抗「毁灭」之前，还有什么比这次合作更能展示我们彼此诚意的？
t_en: Apart from this cooperation, what better proof of sincerity could there be before we join forces against the Destruction?

t_ch: 成交。我不怀疑：帝弓天将的实力。但，我怀疑：「仙舟第一杀手」真的会悄无声息地「离去」吗？
t_en: Deal. What I do not doubt: the might of an Arbiter General. However, I do doubt: will "the Xianzhou's Number One Assassin" truly "leave" without a commotion?

t_ch: 按照我的推演，即便不考虑「命运的奴隶」对他们的指引，单论逮捕星核猎手个体，其成功率也不足<unbreak>9.8</unbreak>％。
t_en: By my extrapolations, even without accounting for the guidance of Destiny's Slave, the probability of capturing a single Stellaron Hunter is less than <unbreak>9.8</unbreak> percent.

t_ch: 连一成都不到吗？身为卜者的我，却不是这么看待概率的。
t_en: Not even ten percent? As a diviner, that's not how I see probability.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>哪怕成事机会仅有「半成」，在我以身入局时，我总会把它看作「十成十」。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Even if the chance of success is only a coin toss, once I enter the fray myself... I always treat it as a "one-hundred-percent certainty."</color>

t_ch: 哇，这落英缤纷的上学路…充满了青春的气氛啊！值得拍一张照。
t_en: Wow, this path to school, lined with falling blossoms... it's such a youthful feeling! Definitely worth a photo.

t_ch: 确实有不少电视剧在这儿取过景。
t_en: Yes, quite a few TV dramas have been filmed here.

t_ch: 想来应该是那种适合情侣分手、爱人错过的经典电视剧吧？
t_en: I imagine it is perfect for those classic series where couples part ways or lovers miss their chance, right?

t_ch: 没错。从这条路笔直往前走一阵，就到绘世学院了。
t_en: Exactly. Follow this road straight ahead for a while, and you will arrive at Graphia Academy.

t_ch: 听说火花大人要宣布大新闻！
t_en: I heard Sparxie has some big news to announce!

t_ch: 真的吗？
t_en: Really?

t_ch: 呃啊，看起来这位火花小姐实在很有知名度。
t_en: Wow... it seems this Sparxie really is a celebrity.

t_ch: 那个高个的褐发眼镜男是谁啊？
t_en: Who's that tall guy with brown hair and glasses?

t_ch: 新来的老师吧？
t_en: Probably a new teacher?

t_ch: 好帅喔～
t_en: He looks so good~

t_ch: 褐色头发、戴眼镜、高个…怎么听起来像杨叔啊？
t_en: Brown hair, glasses, tall... why does that sound like Mr. Yang?

t_ch: 哎？不可能的吧？杨叔有这么惹人怜爱吗…哈哈？
t_en: Huh? No way. Is Mr. Yang really that lovable... haha?

t_ch: 斯科特同学又顶撞了老师。
t_en: Skott talked back to the teacher again.

t_ch: 那个三年级的黄毛混混？
t_en: Skott? That blond-haired delinquent from third year?

t_ch: 倒也不意外。
t_en: Not a complete surprise.

t_ch: 三月七同学和{NICKNAME}同学，转学手续已经办理完成，请在听到广播后立刻前往校长室。
t_en: March 7th, {NICKNAME}, your transfer procedures have been completed. Please proceed to the dean's office immediately.

t_ch: 转、转学手续？这又是怎么回事啊？
t_en: T—Transfer procedures? What's going on here?

t_ch: 咱们找找吧，去看一眼？
t_en: Let's go take a look, shall we?

t_ch: 学校…学生？这一切到底怎么回事？
t_en: School... students? What in the world is going on here?

t_ch: 想办的事还挺多，该从哪件做起呢？
t_en: I have so many things I want to do. Where should I start?

t_ch: 是去上课？还是去应付斯科特？还是…到处逛逛？
t_en: Should I head to class, deal with Skott, or... just wander around?

t_ch: 是我眼花了吗？怎么还有一个我？
t_en: Am I seeing things? Why is there another me?

t_ch: 斯科特说，要在天台和我决斗。
t_en: Skott said he wants to duel me on the rooftop.

t_ch: 不如先抄小道上去，看看有没有埋伏？
t_en: Why not take the side path up and see if there's an ambush?

t_ch: 不教训你这个白痴，实在难消我心头之恨！
t_en: I cannot rest until I teach an idiot like you a lesson!

t_ch: 兄弟姐妹们给我上啊！
t_en: All of you, charge!

t_ch: 等等，你怎么不亲自上场啊斯科特？
t_en: Hey, why aren't you getting in on this yourself, Skott?

t_ch: 这是正义的围殴！你到底懂不懂决斗的法则！
t_en: This is justice by numbers! Do you even understand the rules of a duel!?

t_ch: 虽然啵啵娃老师躲了起来，但我们可以拍下她残留的记忆。
t_en: Even though Ms. Dollie is in hiding, we can still capture her lingering memories.

t_ch: 跟着这些忆灵的踪迹…
t_en: Follow the trail of these memosprites...

t_ch: 很近了…这儿有啵啵娃老师的记忆碎片。
t_en: We're getting real close... There is a fragment of Ms. Dollie's memory here.

t_ch: 教室的门…果然锁起来了。
t_en: The door to this classroom... it's locked, just as I thought.

t_ch: 找找其他入口吧。
t_en: Better look for another way in.

t_ch: 可以跳得更高啦！
t_en: I can jump even higher now!

t_ch: 粉丝们~为我怦然心动，为我争斗吧！
t_en: My dear fans, be charmed by me! Fight for me!

t_ch: 把你们的欢喜、忧愁、痴迷，统统献给我…
t_en: Gimme all your joy, sorrow, and obsession! Gimme every last drop of it...

t_ch: …把「愿力」给我！！
t_en: ...Gimme your "Wishpower"!!!

t_ch: 这些粉丝…不是一般的厉害！
t_en: These fans... they're far too strong!

t_ch: 糟了，这样下去——哎，你是哪位老师？
t_en: This is bad, if this continues... wait, who are you?

t_ch: 啊，好可惜，丹恒同学还是走了……
t_en: Ah, what a shame... Dan Heng's gone after all...

t_ch: 话说利索了，是转学走了。
t_en: Whoa, easy there. He got transferred.

t_ch: 我还没来得及向他告白呢……
t_en: I didn't even get the chance to confess my feelings...

t_ch: 听说他就是收了太多告白信不胜其扰转学走的。
t_en: Word is he chose to transfer 'cause he got so swamped with so many love letters he just couldn't deal with them anymore.

t_ch: 看到了吗，{NICKNAME}和{F#她}{M#他}的宿敌又吵起来了。
t_en: See that? {NICKNAME} and {F#her}{M#his} arch-nemesis are at it again.

t_ch: 估计是为了棒球社的事情吧？
t_en: Probably about the Baseball Club, right?

t_ch: 谁知道呢，也许是情感上的宿敌也说不定…听说斯科特这小子最近心有所属了。
t_en: Who knows? Maybe they're romantic rivals or something... I heard Skott's got his eye on someone lately.

t_ch: 我知道！你说的是隔壁班的拉克什米！
t_en: I know! You're talking about Rakshmi from the class next door!

t_ch: 与此同时，二维市某处……
t_en: Meanwhile, somewhere in Duomension City...

t_ch: 你的心声告诉我，对于我，你仍有疑虑。
t_en: Your thoughts tell me that you still have doubts about me.

t_ch: 约在此地碰头，是想让我为你解惑一二？
t_en: Did you ask to meet here so I could clear up some of your doubts?

t_ch: …爻光大人。
t_en: ...Greetings, Lady Yao Guang.

t_ch: 能随时倾听对方的心思，「调律」这个能力还真是方便啊。
t_en: Being able to hear others' thoughts anytime... this "tuning" ability sure comes in handy.

t_ch: 和戎韬将军来去无踪的本事一比，不过是些微末小道罢了。
t_en: Compared to the Seer Strategist's ability to come and go as she pleases, this is nothing but a parlor trick.

t_ch: 久闻仙舟人看重礼数，我已到了，将军却还是真人不露相，未免有些失礼了吧？
t_en: I've heard that Xianzhou folks are big on etiquette. Yet here I am, and you're still playing hide-and-seek. General, isn't that a bit discourteous?

t_ch: 抱歉…我总会突然消失这个老毛病，实在是身不由己。
t_en: Sorry... I have this old problem of suddenly disappearing. It's really something beyond my control.

t_ch: 至于原因，恕我无法透露。失礼之处，还请多多包涵。
t_en: As for the reason, I'm afraid I can't disclose it. Please forgive my rudeness.

t_ch: 又或者，你要是真的好奇，也可以用「调律」自己来寻找答案啊？
t_en: Or, if you're truly that curious, why not use your "tuning" and find out?

t_ch: 你知道我不会这么做的。
t_en: You know I won't do that.

t_ch: 何况，眼下我也办不到。
t_en: Besides, I couldn't even if I wanted to right now.

t_ch: 办不到？
t_en: Couldn't?

t_ch: 此时，四周人群的思想就像海潮一样涌向我。
t_en: At this moment, the crowd's thoughts are crashing over me like a tidal wave.

t_ch: 我没法隔绝这些杂讯，光是束缚自己的念头不让它们卷入别人的意识，就已精疲力竭了。
t_en: I can't block out all this mental noise. Just keeping my own thoughts from getting tangled up in everyone else's is hard enough.

t_ch: 原来如此，所以刚才和姬子小姐会谈时，我闯入了你们俩的「私人通信」，也是这么回事吧。
t_en: I see. So that's why, when you were speaking with Miss Himeko earlier, I accidentally intruded on your "private conversation."

t_ch: 我的「调律」正在失控。这副面具…扭曲了我的能力。
t_en: My "tuning" is spiraling out of control. This mask... is distorting my abilities.

t_ch: …我却不这么看，失控是暂时的。那副面具在增强你的力量。
t_en: ...I see it differently. The loss of control is temporary. That mask is amplifying your power.

t_ch: 这怎么可能？「同谐」赐下的调律之力，「欢愉」如何能擅动祂的权能？
t_en: How is this possible? This "tuning" power is bestowed by the Harmony. How could the Elation meddle with THEIR authority?

t_ch: 也许，「欢愉」只是为这份权能注入了更多「燃料」也说不定呢？
t_en: Perhaps the Elation simply poured more "fuel" into this divine authority?

t_ch: 依我愚见，<color=#dbc291ff>这张面具将谒者和二相乐园的众生联为一体，抽汲此间相同命途的力量，供你们使用。</color>
t_en: In my humble opinion, <color=#dbc291ff>this mask binds the Supplicant to all beings of Planarcadia, drawing strength from those who share the same Path here for your use.</color>

t_ch: 这就是你能听到别人的心声如海潮般涌向你的原因…你的「调律」正在不断扩张，你已成了这曲旋律的中心。
t_en: That's why you're hearing others' thoughts surge toward you like a tide... Your "tuning" is steadily expanding, and you've become the center of this melody.

t_ch: 将军还真是送了我一份「重礼」啊。
t_en: You sure gave me quite the "gift," general.

t_ch: 只是如此贵重的礼物，为何却偏要扣在我的脸上…我需要一个坦诚回答。
t_en: But why would such a precious gift be placed on my face...? I need an honest answer.

t_ch: 理由？我之前不是解释了吗？
t_en: You want to know the reason? Didn't I explain it already?

t_ch: 与我有关的理由，言之成理。但我更想听的是，与你自己有关的答案。
t_en: Your reasons about me make sense, but what I really want is your answer... about yourself.

t_ch: 洞察未来的玉阙将军，为何不把这副面具扣在自己脸上，靠着它给予的力量，亲身入局？
t_en: General of the Yuque who sees into the future... why not wear the mask yourself and personally step into the fray with its power?

t_ch: 言语可以虚饰伪装，唯有「调律」才能揭开真相。
t_en: Words can deceive, but only "tuning" lays the truth bare.

t_ch: 星期日先生，不妨用你被增强过的「调律」力量，亲自体会这个答案的重量吧。
t_en: Mr. Sunday, why not use your enhanced "tuning" to feel the weight of that answer for yourself?

t_ch: 那就…冒犯了，爻光大人。
t_en: Well then... pardon my intrusion, Lady Yao Guang.

t_ch: 啊…这只停在我肩上的鸟儿，就是「调律」感应之力的具现？
t_en: Ah... so this bird on my shoulder is the manifestation of the "tuning's" resonance?

t_ch: 正是。而我能看到的这些人头顶的符号……
t_en: Exactly. And these symbols I see floating above people's heads...

t_ch: …也就是将军所说的「吉凶签」？
t_en: ...Would be the "Weal and Woe Signs" you mentioned, general?

t_ch: 没错。成为帝弓天将，须得经历「天虹封典」、「帝弓垂眸」以及「受赐威灵」三道仪式，如此方能算得上接掌符节，名实相称。
t_en: That's right. To become an Arbiter-General, one must undergo three ceremonies: the "Rainbow Ordainment," the "Reignbow's Gaze," and the "Bestowal of Spiritus." Only then can one truly take up the mantle and live up to the title.

t_ch: 我接任玉阙将军时受赐的帝弓威灵，名唤「时轮天雉明王」，于帝弓七相中代表「巡猎」的眼界，是预视、决断与自制的象征。
t_en: When I was appointed as the Yuque's general, I was bestowed with the Reignbow Spiritus called "Kālacakra Skyplume," which represents the vision of the Hunt among the seven aspects of the Reignbow: a symbol of foresight, verdict, and self-discipline.

t_ch: 正是靠着它，我才拥有了判读气运的「观自在眼」。
t_en: Thanks to that, I gained the "Eye of Īśvara," which allows me to read the flow of destiny.

t_ch: 自抵达这个世界之日起，我举目所见，往来行人皆头顶赤红的「凶签」，无一例外。最迟不过数日，将有一场灭顶灾变到来。
t_en: Since the day I arrived on this world, every person I've seen bore a crimson "Woe Sign" above their head without exception. Within days at most, a catastrophic disaster will befall this place.

t_ch: 所以，我看到城市远方升起的这蓬红光是……？
t_en: So, that red glow rising from the edge of the city is...?

t_ch: 那是二相乐园所有人的「凶签」汇聚而成的光焰。
t_en: The blazing light formed by the convergence of all those "Woe Signs" in Planarcadia.

t_ch: 这么说来，将军更应佩戴面具才对——您洞知未来的能力一经增强，便不必畏惧占卜结果中的「死兆」了。
t_en: In that case, general, it's all the more reason for you to wear the mask. Once your sight into the future is strengthened, you won't need to fear the "deathly omen" in your readings.

t_ch: 星期日先生，卜者有一条「事不二卜」的铁律，不知你是否听过？
t_en: Mr. Sunday, have you heard the diviners' iron law? "Never divine the same matter twice"?

t_ch: 匹诺康尼的占卜者用卡牌占卜前也会告诫顾客，不能因为占卜结果不顺心意，就要再来一次。这其中难道有什么讲究？
t_en: In Penacony, diviners also warn clients beforehand: You can't ask for another spread just because you dislike the result. There's a reason for that, isn't there?

t_ch: 占卜未来，本质上是一种观测与计算，是对未来的干涉。而每一次测算，都会将混沌不清的未来推向某个确定的坍缩结果，即便这结果并不为人所乐见。
t_en: Divining the future is, in essence, a form of observation and calculation. It's an interference with the future. Each reading drives the murky future toward a definite outcome, a collapsed quantum state, even if that outcome isn't to one's liking.

t_ch: 不瞒你说，动身前往这二相乐园时，本座确实做好了亲自踏入「幻月游戏」的打算。
t_en: To be honest, when I set out for Planarcadia, I had fully intended to personally participate in the Phantasmoon Games.

t_ch: 但在面具入手的瞬间，我已明了佩戴它的后果——这件奇物一旦加身，「观自在眼」将超脱目光所限，遍入此界，时轮天雉明王会以每秒百亿次计判读众生的未来。
t_en: But the moment I laid hands on the mask, I understood the consequences of wearing it. Once this miraculous item is donned, the "Eye of Īśvara" would transcend mere sight and permeate throughout this realm. The Kālacakra Skyplume would predict the future of all beings at tens of billions of instances per second.

t_ch: …你是说，只是靠你双眼观察，我们的未来就会被决定？
t_en: ...You're saying that, simply by your observation, our future would become fixed?

t_ch: 不错，就像{RUBY_B#博识尊}遍智天君{RUBY_E#}阐演天道，锚下命定的时刻。
t_en: Indeed, just like how {RUBY_B#Nous}the Wisdomwalker{RUBY_E#} expounded the way of destiny, anchoring the fated moments.

t_ch: 如果戴上面具，我就成了这二相乐园之上的博识尊，我会「亲眼」将诸位推入黑暗的命运，囚锁在命定的死局里，再无一点拯救的希望。
t_en: If I wore the mask, I would become the Nous of Planarcadia, and I would "personally anchor" a destiny that drives us all into a bottomless darkness. We will become locked in a dead end with not a shred of hope for salvation.

t_ch: 好险，我通过了考验。这一局，尚有一线生机。
t_en: Close one. I passed the trial. There's still a sliver of hope in this round.

t_ch: …这就更让我感到好奇了。
t_en: ...Now that makes me even more curious.

t_ch: 恕我失礼，二相乐园亿万生灵的吉凶命运，和将军本来也没什么关系。列车、公司与你只是一面之缘。
t_en: Pardon my bluntness, but the fate of billions in Planarcadia never had much to do with you, General. Your encounter with the Express and the IPC was merely a chance meeting.

t_ch: 你是仙舟的使者，既然预见了「一位帝弓天将在此殒落」的征兆，你就该随时登船撤离……
t_en: You are a messenger of the Xianzhou. Since you foresaw the omen — that "an Arbiter General will fall here" — you could have boarded your ship and left at any time...

t_ch: 为什么要选择留下来帮助我们，帮助这个世界挣脱眼前的死局？
t_en: Why choose to stay and help us? To help this world break free of this deathtrap?

t_ch: 那是因为…本座心怀大爱，慈悲为怀，只因每一条生命都弥足珍贵——
t_en: Because... I am guided by great compassion. Every life is precious beyond measure...

t_ch: 这些个场面话，想来骗不了你这位匹诺康尼前主公吧？
t_en: Such diplomatic pleasantries won't fool the former master of Penacony like yourself, would they?

t_ch: 那你看看这个理由怎么样？星穹列车抗击「毁灭」，屡建奇功，于未来肩负不可或缺的重任，我绝不能坐看诸位陷入灭顶之灾——
t_en: Then, how about this reason? The Astral Express has distinguished itself time and again against the Destruction, and bears an irreplaceable duty for the future. I can't just sit idly and watch you face annihilation...

t_ch: 唉，饶了我吧，本座真的不擅长外交辞令啊。
t_en: *sigh* Give me a break. I really am terrible at all this diplomatic talk.

t_ch: 星期日先生，是什么让你觉得，我所做的一切，只是为了拯救二相乐园？
t_en: Mr. Sunday, what makes you think that everything I've done is just to save Planarcadia?

t_ch: 什么？
t_en: What?

t_ch: 「福兮，祸之所伏；祸兮，福之所倚。」一部分人的吉运，往往导致另一些人的凶命；而一部分人面临的死局，也许潜藏着另一群人的生机。
t_en: "Weal is where Woe lurks. Woe is where Weal leans." The good fortune of some often births the ill fate of others, and a dead end for someone may hide an opportunity for another.

t_ch: 这个生死百转、否泰错变的凶危之地，在我眼中恰恰也是绝佳的博弈之地。
t_en: This treacherous land where life and death turn again and again, where fortune flips to misfortune, is, in my eyes, the perfect board for a game of wits.

t_ch: 与谁博弈？
t_en: A game with whom?

t_ch: …与神。
t_en: ...With the gods.

t_ch: 不知星期日先生是否听过三十年前的「方壶血战」？
t_en: Mr. Sunday, have you heard of the "Battle of the Fanghu" thirty years ago?

t_ch: 我确实听过一些战争的传闻，但对细节所知不多。将军是想说，此刻的二相乐园，就和当年的方壶仙舟一样凶险？
t_en: I've heard rumors of that war, but not the details. General, are you suggesting Planarcadia is now facing dangers just as perilous as the Xianzhou Fanghu was back then?

t_ch: 你没猜错。
t_en: You got that right.

t_ch: 三十年前，丰饶孽物的联军撕开阵线，杀入了方壶本土。孽物们唤醒了一颗活化的妖星，意欲击沉方壶仙舟。
t_en: Thirty years ago, the Abundance Axis broke our lines and invaded the Fanghu homeland. The abominations awakened a living demonic star, intending to sink the Xianzhou Fanghu.

t_ch: 联盟无计可施，遂兵行险着，采纳了我师妹提出的计划，操纵观星器具「瞰云镜」向帝弓发射信号求援，俟其光矢击落妖星。
t_en: With no other options, the Alliance staked everything on my junior soror's plan... to operate the astronomical observation device, the Cloudpeer Telescope, to signal the Reignbow for aid, using THEIR Lux Arrow to strike down the demon star.

t_ch: 而负责操作「瞰云镜」的那人将有去无回，十死无生。
t_en: Whoever operated the Cloudpeer Telescope would not return. It was to be a one-way trip.

t_ch: 莫非将军的师妹……？
t_en: So your junior soror...?

t_ch: 您猜错了。那位自愿成仁者，是教我卜筮之道的师父。时任玉阙太卜的竟天先生，是唯一有权限操作那件器物的重臣。他接受天命，前往「方壶」。
t_en: You guessed wrong. The one who offered his life was my master, who taught me divination. It was Master Jingtian, then the Yuque's Master Diviner, and the only high official authorized to operate that device. He accepted his fate and ventured to the Fanghu.

t_ch: 与他一同踏入死地的，还有彼时护卫「瞰云镜」的前代曜青将军月御，以及她所率领的青丘军士卒。
t_en: Going with him into certain death were Yueyu, the previous Yaoqing General who guarded the Cloudpeer Telescope, and the Verdant Knights under her command.

t_ch: 而我负责勘定整个计划的可行性，补足其缺漏。我启用「十方光映法界」，一次次计算，一次次推占。
t_en: I was responsible for assessing the entire plan's feasibility and mending its gaps. I activated the Deca-Light Reflection Barrier, calculating and casting lots again and again.

t_ch: 我目睹着代表众多可能性的「星光」于法界中闪烁…微弱明灭，终至被一位神明沉思的巨影吞没，消失在天渊深邃的黑暗里。
t_en: I watched as the "starlight" representing countless possibilities flickered within the reflections — faint, wavering — until the vast shadow of a pondering god swallowed them whole, and they vanished into the abyssal dark at the edges of the universe.

t_ch: 至此，我明白，我已将师父和万千军士的性命砌进了无从逃脱的死路。光矢坠下时，他们将与敌人…还有那颗妖星一同灰飞烟灭。
t_en: In that moment, I understood that I had cemented my master and countless soldiers into an inescapable doom. When the Lux Arrow fell, they would perish along with the enemy and that demonic star... all reduced to ashes.

t_ch: 你们牺牲了许多战友，只为确保敌人的死亡？
t_en: You sacrificed so many comrades just to ensure the enemy's fall?

t_ch: 在匹诺康尼人听来，这样的事情一定很残忍吧？
t_en: To Penaconians, that must seem incredibly cruel, right?

t_ch: …诞生在和平年代的我无权置评。也许匹诺康尼的创立者们能理解其中的无奈。
t_en: ...As someone born in an era of peace, I have no right to judge. Perhaps the founders of Penacony could understand the necessity.

t_ch: 无论残忍与否，这都是我的职责。因此，我也必须见证并铭记他们的牺牲。
t_en: Cruel or not, it was my duty. And so I had to witness, and remember their sacrifice.

t_ch: 在目睹光矢坠下的那一刻，我意识到，<color=#dbc291ff>仙舟联盟竟然牵引了一位星神的行动轨迹。</color>
t_en: When I saw the Lux Arrow descend, I realized <color=#dbc291ff>the Xianzhou Alliance had actually directed an Aeon's actions.</color>

t_ch: 这个念头照亮了我余生的使命。在这冰冷无情的银河里，要想从绝望死局中争得一线生机，星神的介入往往是唯一的希望。
t_en: This realization illuminated the mission of my remaining years. In this cold, merciless cosmos, when you're trapped in despair, an Aeon's intervention is often the only hope.

t_ch: 我们并非目视{RUBY_B#星神}天体{RUBY_E#}运行却无能为力的蝼蚁。是我们献出自己微不足道的命运的一部分，铸就了{RUBY_B#命途}祂们的轨迹{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: We are not ants, helpless beneath the celestial spheres as we simply watch the {RUBY_B#Aeons}stars{RUBY_E#} turn. Instead, {RUBY_B#Paths}THEIR trails{RUBY_E#} were forged by us, who offered up a portion of our insignificant destiny.

t_ch: 伟大如星神，也可以被我们的意志所牵引！
t_en: Even beings as mighty as the Aeons can be pulled by our will!

t_ch: …这就是你想在二相乐园做的事？
t_en: ...Is this what you want to do in Planarcadia?

t_ch: 不错。我要握住那些曾被我亲手埋葬的「可能性的星光」，一如三十年前的方壶之战，一位天将陨落，一尊星神垂迹。
t_en: Indeed. I will seize those "starlights of possibility" I once buried with my own hands. Just like the Battle of the Fanghu thirty years ago: an Arbiter-General will fall, and an Aeon will descend.

t_ch: 我要将命定的死兆铸为算筹，我要邀请那位{RUBY_B#阿哈}钟情拨弄命运的神明{RUBY_E#}入局。
t_en: I will forge the destined death into wagers and game pieces, and invite {RUBY_B#Aha}the god who delights in meddling with destiny{RUBY_E#} to sit at my table.

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>这一次，我要请祂…入我卦中，为银河的未来摇出大吉之签！</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>This time, I will invite THEM into my calculations... to draw a Great Weal for the cosmos's future!</color>

t_ch: 星期日先生，这一念狂想，曾登临{RUBY_B#太一}「秩序」{RUBY_E#}神座的你，想必也能够理解吧？
t_en: Mr. Sunday, surely you can understand this mad idea of mine, having once reached the divine throne of {RUBY_B#Ena}Order{RUBY_E#} yourself?

t_ch: …我们的共同点，绝不止「狂想」这一处。
t_en: Our common ground goes far beyond "mad ideas."

t_ch: 只是，我复苏的是一位在深渊中瞑目已死的神明。将军又怎么能确定，那位最善变的星神愿意被你的狂想牵引？
t_en: But I was reviving a god who already lay dead and buried in the infinite dark. General... how can you be sure that the most capricious Aeon would be led by your fancy?

t_ch: 你听说过这样一则「欢愉」的神话吗？
t_en: Have you heard this myth about the Elation?

t_ch: 传闻阿哈曾攀上存在之树的高枝，祂放眼四望，只见星辰冰冷运转如机器，一切的意义让位于虚无…唯独一名婴儿降生的啼哭却引来祂的哈哈大笑。
t_en: They say Aha once climbed to the highest bough of the Tree of Existence. Gazing out, THEY saw stars turning coldly like machines, and the meaning of everything yielded to nihility... Only the cry of a newborn made THEM burst into laughter.

t_ch: 星期日先生，请回答我：阿哈因何而欢笑？
t_en: Mr. Sunday, answer me: why did Aha laugh?

t_ch: 我想是因为，祂终于看到一位观众来到了宇宙这个空旷许久的舞台前。
t_en: I think it's because THEY finally saw an audience arrive at this long-empty stage we call the universe.

t_ch: 答得好啊！你比外表看起来要幽默多了嘛！
t_en: Well said! You're way funnier than you look!

t_ch: 卜筮之道有云「天衍四九，人遁其一」。我们是这恒盲无智的宇宙所能创造出的最大的变数。
t_en: As divination teaches: "Heaven creates forty-nine paths. Humans find the one unseen." WE are the greatest variable this blind, mindless universe can produce.

t_ch: 宇宙与众神为我们设谜，而我们将会给祂们意外…一个「终末」以外的答案。
t_en: The universe and the gods set riddles for us, and we shall give THEM a surprise... An answer beyond the Finality.

t_ch: 啊，时间差不多了……
t_en: Ah, looks like it's about time...

t_ch: 莫非，将军又要不告而别了？
t_en: Don't tell me you're disappearing without a goodbye again, general?

t_ch: 不，这一次，我们俩要将目光投向同一个地方，「亲眼」从命运中凿开一线可能。
t_en: No. This time, we will set our eyes on the same place and carve a sliver of possibility out of destiny with our own "sight."

t_ch: 您说的该不会是…姬子{F#她们}{M#他们}前往的那所学校？
t_en: Could you be talking about... the school where Himeko and the others went?

t_ch: 嗯，今日那儿赤光烛天，正是一处「凶位」。乐园的第一场灾变，将会从那儿爆发。
t_en: Yes. The crimson light there pierces the sky today, marking it as a locus of Woe. The arcadia's first calamity will erupt there.

t_ch: 早知道该在他们出发前，求将军给他们几个「锦囊妙计」啊……
t_en: I should've asked you to give them a few "secret strategies" before they left...

t_ch: 算了，反正写的都是些无关痛痒的说笑打趣…就饶了他们吧。
t_en: Forget it. The stuff in those pouches is all harmless banter anyway... We should let them off the hook.

t_ch: 大家好，欢迎回到满愿电视台《乐园早知道》特别新闻栏目！我是主持人噗噗！
t_en: Hello everyone, welcome back to Fulwish TV's special news segment, Arcadian Morning Report! I'm your host, Poofy!

t_ch: 大家好，我是主持人雪雪！
t_en: Hi everyone, I'm your host, Snowie!

t_ch: 距离第一位谒者火花小姐宣布加入幻月游戏已经过去了三小时。她向所有粉丝们宣告的演出——「火花大会」也即将到来！
t_en: It's been three hours since our first Supplicant, Miss Sparxie, announced her entry into the Phantasmoon Games. The performance she announced to all her fans, SparxiCon, is also about to begin!

t_ch: 真让人好奇啊，二相乐园的昼夜节律早在古老的时代里就被完全打乱了！绘世学院所处的区域恰好是个光照充沛的地方……
t_en: How curious! Planarcadia's day–night cycle was completely scrambled in ancient times! Graphia Academy's location just happens to be bathed in light...

t_ch: 所以，在明晃晃的白昼里，火花小姐到底要为我们呈现一出怎样的烟花表演呢？
t_en: So, in broad daylight, what kind of fireworks show does Miss Sparxie have in store for us?

t_ch: 谁知道呢？该不会是要把学校炸个大烟花吧？毕竟十五年前的幻月游戏，也是在绘世学院里发生了——
t_en: Who knows? Maybe she'll blow the school sky-high in a blaze of fireworks? After all, at the Phantasmoon Games fifteen years ago, Graphia Academy also...

t_ch: 哎，前辈，这样的玩笑可开不得啊！各位观众，非常抱歉！下面让我们来播报有关其他谒者的消息。
t_en: Hey, senior, you can't joke about that! Dear viewers, our apologies! Let's move on to news about other Supplicants.

t_ch: 根据异常防御部的工作人员提供的消息，在例行检查幻月秘庭时，他们发现<color=#dbc291ff>神坛上显现出另外两张面具的形状。</color>
t_en: According to the Department of Aberration Defense's staff, during a routine inspection of the Phantasmoon Courtyard, <color=#dbc291ff>two additional mask shapes manifested on the altar</color>.

t_ch: 也就是说，<color=#dbc291ff>又有两位谒者出现了！</color>
t_en: In other words, <color=#dbc291ff>two more Supplicants have appeared!</color>

t_ch: 虽然截至目前为止，尚未有任何谒者公开宣布加入游戏，但神坛上的面具代表有人在某处悄然佩戴上了「欢愉假面」。
t_en: Although none of them have publicly announced entry into the games yet, the altar's masks mean someone, somewhere, has quietly donned the "Masks of Elation."

t_ch: 关于这两副面具，按照异常防御部工作人员的描述，其中之一是浣熊的形象，另一副似乎是某种鸟类……
t_en: As for the two masks, Department of Aberration Defense staff describe one as a raccoon and the other as some kind of bird...

t_ch: 浣熊面具啊…不免让人想起最近抵达二相乐园的星穹列车。
t_en: A raccoon mask, huh... Hard not to think of the Astral Express that just arrived in Planarcadia.

t_ch: 星穹列车的无名客会加入这次的幻月游戏吗？听说「火花大会」也邀请了他们作为嘉宾呢！
t_en: Will the Nameless from the Astral Express join the Phantasmoon Games? I hear they've even invited them to SparxiCon as special guests!

t_ch: 嗯！这到底会是一场谒者间联手创造的奇迹秀，还是两位名动一方的谒者大展身手，彼此对抗呢？
t_en: Hmm! Will it be a miraculous show formed out of the collaboration between Supplicants, or a head-to-head showcase between two famed contenders?

t_ch: 此时已经有不计其数的主播正在直播现场盛况，让我们把画面转到现场的直播中——
t_en: Countless streamers are already live, broadcasting the scene at the venue, let's cut to the feed now...

t_ch: 啊！快看，是星穹列车的无名客来了！
t_en: Ah! Look, it's the Nameless from the Astral Express!

t_ch: 一人千面，千面一人~欢迎来到「火花大会」直播现场！
t_en: One girl, a thousand faces! A thousand faces, one girl! Welcome to the SparxiCon live broadcast!

t_ch: 请各位可爱的粉丝耐心等待~♥，「火花大会」即将正式开场！
t_en: Dear lovely fans, please be patient ♥! SparxiCon is about to begin!

t_ch: 这场恐怖直播马上就要结束了。你们也该下课了。
t_en: This horror stream is about to end. Time for you all to log off.

t_ch: 这个女人竟然长着火花大人的脸！好拙劣的模仿！
t_en: That woman has Miss Sparxie's face! What a pathetic imitation!

t_ch: 什么？我的脸才是原创！
t_en: What? My face is the original!

t_ch: 只有火花大人的脸才是世上最伟大的作品！
t_en: Miss Sparxie's face alone is the greatest masterpiece in the world!

t_ch: 无论是谁，都应该聆听她甜美的声音，仰望她可爱的脸庞！
t_en: Everyone should listen to her sweet voice and gaze upon her adorable face!

t_ch: 我们要成为火花大人~！
t_en: We shall become Miss Sparxie!

t_ch: 把一切献给火花大人~！
t_en: We offer everything to Miss Sparxie~!

t_ch: 火花花！火花花！火花花！——
t_en: Sparxicle! Sparxicle! Sparxicle!

t_ch: 呜…我的眼睛！看到脏东西了！
t_en: Ugh... my eyes! I've seen something cursed!

t_ch: 这里模因病毒人传人的现象比想像得要严重多了。地上都长出会走路的火花菇来了…怕是跑不了几步，我们几个就会变成火花脸…呃，也许该说花火脸？
t_en: The memetic virus spread among people is way worse than we imagined. There are walking Sparxie-shrooms growing on the ground... I bet we'll all end up with Sparxie... or rather, Sparkle faces before we can get very far.

t_ch: 掏出模因香蕉。
t_en: Take out the memetic banana.

t_ch: 真的要用上它吗？在病毒发作前要是不能解决那个冒牌货，咱们就都得去原始森林里当猴儿了。
t_en: Are we really using that? If we don't take down that impostor before the virus hits, we'll all be monkeys in the jungle.

t_ch: 是成为自己冒牌货的冒牌货，还是做一只无忧无虑的猴子，你自己选吧。
t_en: Become a fake of a fake, or a carefree monkey. Your call.

t_ch: 比起变成猴子，总觉得被冒牌货同化似乎也不是那么糟糕啊……
t_en: On second thought... being assimilated by an impostor doesn't sound that bad...

t_ch: 少废话，看蕉。
t_en: Enough talk. Eyes on the banana.

t_ch: 哎呀！…蕉！大姐头，用「香蕉」扎人的时候能不能提个醒啊！
t_en: Ow! ...Bana! Ma'am, a little warning before you stab someone with a banana, please!

t_ch: 来吧！蕉！
t_en: Come on! Bana!

t_ch: 还记得我们在酒馆里制定的计划吗？
t_en: Remember the plan we made at the tavern?

t_ch: 嗯，蕉出…我是说，翻出被火花藏起来的那座幻造景画，用「浪花」冲刷整座学蕉…我是说，绘世学院！
t_en: Yes bana! ...I mean, we dig out the imagenated painting Sparxie hid, then use "waves" to give Graphia Academy a good banana-over... I mean, wash-over!

t_ch: 但是…那个冒牌货要是发现了我们的计划怎么办？
t_en: But... what if that impostor figures it out?

t_ch: 尽力拖住她，为我们争取时间。
t_en: Just try to keep her busy and buy us some time.

t_ch: 就这么办！
t_en: Let's do it!

t_ch: 哟吼~嘉宾们，你们可算来啦！我可是等到手机都快没电了唷~
t_en: Yoohoooo! Welcome, dear guests! I've been waiting so long my phone's about to die!

t_ch: 收手吧，可爱的面具~我不该用「冒牌货」这样伤人的字眼来叫你。
t_en: Drop the act, my dear mask. I shouldn't have called you an "impostor." That was harsh of me.

t_ch: 回到妈妈怀里，一切还来得及。真要让星穹列车的人动起手来，他们可没轻没重的。
t_en: Come back to mama's arms. It's not too late. If the Astral Express crew gets involved, they won't be pulling any punches.

t_ch: 当然，我明白，现在投降可就太浪费你精心布置的舞台，也太毁节目效果了，对不对？
t_en: Of course, I get it. Surrendering now would ruin the stage you so carefully set. Total buzzkill, right?

t_ch: 噗，哈哈哈哈…所以，你们还是什么都不明白！
t_en: Pfft, hahaha... So you still don't get it!

t_ch: 「一个演员最不该做的，就是让自己的存在感被捆绑在某一类角色上。」
t_en: "The worst thing an actor should do is be tied down to one type of role."

t_ch: 站在你们面前的这位，一路相伴你们左右的忠实朋友，从没耍弄任何诡计的「假面愚者」……
t_en: Standing before you, the loyal friend who's been by your side all along, the Masked Fool who never played any tricks...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>她，才是被我抛弃的旧面具。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>SHE is actually the old mask I have shed.</color>

t_ch: 你说什么？！
t_en: What did you just say!?

t_ch: 你才是花火本尊？！
t_en: YOU are the real Sparkle!?

t_ch: 我不是一直在反复告诉你们，她只是个「旧、型、号」吗？
t_en: Haven't I been telling you all this time? She's just an OLD MODEL.

t_ch: 也难怪「酒馆」里的灯球对花火毫无反应。
t_en: No wonder the light orb in the tavern didn't react to Sparkle.

t_ch: …真的假的？！这说法…太带感了！
t_en: ...For real!? That explanation is... kinda awesome!

t_ch: 看看你们脸上的表情，这简直是「火花大会」最棒的开幕秀啊！
t_en: Look at those expressions on your faces! This is the best opening act for SparxiCon ever!

t_ch: 我蜕下旧日的面具，也脱下了过去那个软弱、畏首畏尾、充满人性的我。
t_en: I shed that old mask, and with it, the weak, timid, all-too-human me.

t_ch: 而这全新的我，将摆脱一切桎梏，汇聚粉丝们的愿力，踏上「幻月游戏」的舞台。
t_en: This new me will break free from all shackles, gather my fans' Wishpower, and stride onto the stage of the Phantasmoon Games.

t_ch: 作为对乐园众生献出愿力的回报，每一位谒者都要向他们展示「何谓欢愉」——展示他们想要的「欢愉」！
t_en: In return for the Wishpower offered by everyone in the arcadia, every Supplicant must show them "what elation truly means." We will demonstrate the Elation we desire!

t_ch: 把所有人变成你？
t_en: By turning everyone into you?

t_ch: 这到底哪里欢愉了？
t_en: How is this even remotely elating?

t_ch: 整个过程中可没有半点强迫，只有「是」和「否」的选择——就像把用户协议拉到底那样。
t_en: There's no coercion involved anywhere in this. It's a simple "Yes" or "No," like scrolling to the bottom of the Terms of Service.

t_ch: 想受人关注吗？想多才多艺吗？想日进斗金吗？想被所有人追捧赞颂吗？那就下载「火花病毒」，噔噔咚，点击「确认」就好。
t_en: Want attention? Talent? Quick cash? Universal praise? Download the "Sparxie Virus"! Ta-da. Click "Confirm," and you're set.

t_ch: 这不正是他们渴望的、三分钟速成的「欢愉」吗？
t_en: Isn't that exactly what they crave? Elation in three minutes flat?

t_ch: 无名客，你们输定了，因为你们根本无法阻挡人们心中的渴望——
t_en: Nameless, you're doomed to lose. You can't stop what people desire...

t_ch: 我们当然没法阻挡人心中的渴望，但是我们会在这场直播里，向这个乐园世界的观众们展示另一种可能。
t_en: We can't stop their desire, but in this stream, we'll show this arcadia world another possibility.

t_ch: 你说什么？
t_en: What did you just say?

t_ch: 我们会向所有观看直播的人们展示：星穹列车的「欢愉」。
t_en: We'll show everyone watching the livestream the Elation of the Astral Express.

t_ch: 我可不是嘉宾，我是谒者！
t_en: I'm not a guest. I'm a Supplicant!

t_ch: 你竟然…得到了另一张面具？！
t_en: You... got another mask!?

t_ch: 「在火花大会举行前，列车的英雄击败了试图散播病毒的邪恶反派」——很快，他们会通过直播见证这一切。他们心中也会升起对「开拓」的向往。
t_en: "Before SparxiCon begins, the hero of the Express will defeat the villain who tried to spread the virus." And soon, the world will watch it happen live. In their hearts, they'll long for the Trailblaze too.

t_ch: 你从来都不是观众心目中唯一的可选项啊，火花。
t_en: You were never the only option for the audience, Sparxie.

t_ch: 知道我为什么讨厌「英雄故事」吗？你们总会打着「正义」的幌子，尝试用暴力摧毁一切有趣的计划。
t_en: You know why I can't stand "heroic stories"? You lot always wave the flag of "justice," then stomp all over the fun with brute force.

t_ch: 「欢愉」，无关正义！你们，也还没胜利呢！
t_en: The Elation has nothing to do with justice! And you haven't won yet!

t_ch: 小灰毛，大姐头，你们忘了原定的计划了吗？我来拖住她。
t_en: Ma'am! Gray Hair! Don't tell me you forgot the plan? I'll stall her.

t_ch: 你还真会抢风头！
t_en: Always gotta steal the spotlight, huh?

t_ch: 咱们仨剩下的时间不多了吧？我可是在为你们争取胜算。
t_en: We don't have much time left, do we? I'm trying to give us better odds of winning here.

t_ch: 你能拖住她多久？
t_en: How long can you hold her off?

t_ch: 很久很久。小灰毛，对我有点信心嘛！
t_en: A long, long time. Come on, Gray Hair, have a little faith in me!

t_ch: 我一直有个疑问：谁是本尊，谁是面具…不该由站着的那个决定吗？
t_en: There's one thing I've always wondered: who's the real Sparkle, and who's just a mask... shouldn't the last one standing decide that?

t_ch: 那就从分出胜负开始好了，我会让你成为这场「火花大会」上第一支开幕烟花！
t_en: Then let's settle it right now. I'll make you the opening firework of SparxiCon!

t_ch: 呀啊啊啊啊啊——
t_en: AAAAAHHHHH...

t_ch: 真是不得了！一个和火花大人长得一模一样的女孩突然冲上前，和她打了起来。
t_en: Unbelievable! A girl who looks exactly like Miss Sparxie just rushed in and started throwing punches.

t_ch: 莫非…她是高仿的竞争对手？
t_en: Could she be... a high-grade lookalike rival?

t_ch: 看来，虚拟主播间最高端的战争往往只会用最朴素的方式进行！
t_en: Looks like when it comes to top-tier VStreamer wars, the fanciest fights are fought the old-fashioned way!
I... huh...

t_ch: 喂，朋友们，看这儿！往这儿看~
t_en: Hey, friends! Over here! Look this way!

t_ch: 是…小三月的声音？
t_en: Is that... March's voice?

t_ch: 来这儿，我和杨叔在美术教室！
t_en: Over here! Mr. Yang and I are in the art classroom!

t_ch: 趁花火拖住她的行动，我们先去和小三月会合吧。
t_en: While Sparkle keeps her busy, let's regroup with March first.

t_ch: 两个相似的形体斗到一处，打得昏天黑地，烟雾滚滚，分不清谁又是谁。战团中隐约有声音传来——
t_en: Two similar figures clashed together, battling fiercely amidst swirling smoke and chaos, making it impossible to tell one from the other. From within the fray, faint voices could be heard...

t_ch: 区区一张面具，竟敢嚣张到威胁主人！
t_en: A mere mask dares to threaten its master with such arrogance!?

t_ch: 谁是面具，谁是主人，还没个准呢！
t_en: Who's the mask, and who's the master? That's still up for debate!

t_ch: 姬子姐！{NICKNAME}！你们可算来啦！
t_en: Miss Himeko! {NICKNAME}! You're finally here!

t_ch: 三月，我搬救兵回来啦！
t_en: March, I brought backup!

t_ch: 三月，你没事可太蕉了！
t_en: March, thank goodness you're okay, bana!

t_ch: 小三月，看蕉你没事，我就放心了。
t_en: March, I'm just so happy you're safe, bana.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}逃出学校后，我就猜到{F#她}{M#他}一定会带你来的！所以趁着火花离开，我先找了个教室躲起来。
t_en: After {NICKNAME} escaped the school, I figured {M#he'd}{F#she'd} bring you back. So while Sparxie was away, I found a classroom to hide in.

t_ch: 学校里学生们的样子越来越诡异了，还好本姑娘的心灵纯洁，没受她什么影响。
t_en: The students around here have been acting weirder and weirder... Good thing my heart is pure and untouched, so her tricks don't work on me!

t_ch: 等等，你们俩刚刚说话是不是带了点什么奇怪的口癖？
t_en: Hold up, were you two just talking with some weird verbal tic?

t_ch: 我们选择了以蕉攻毒。
t_en: We chose to fight poison with banana.

t_ch: …什么？为了对抗火花的影响，你们居然用上了匹诺康尼那个模因病毒？
t_en: ...What? You actually used that Penaconian memetic virus to counter Sparxie's influence?

t_ch: 不管列车变成「花」车还是「猴」车，我都不想看到哇！
t_en: I don't care if the Express turns into a "Sparxie" train or a "monkey" train! I want no part of either!

t_ch: 杨叔还好吗？
t_en: Is Mr. Yang okay?

t_ch: 瓦尔特呢？他没事吧？
t_en: What about Welt? Is he alright?

t_ch: 杨叔，是自己人来了！快出来吧！
t_en: Mr. Yang, it's our friends! Come on out!

t_ch: 你看起来也没什么异常嘛？所以{NICKNAME}在新闻上看到的那位瓦尔特「老师」……
t_en: You look perfectly normal, though? So that "Professor" Welt {NICKNAME} saw in the news...

t_ch: 一点小小的演技，先别提这个了。
t_en: Just a bit of acting on my part. Let's not dwell on that right now.

t_ch: 和小三月一样，我来到学校后发现到有人在污染我们的认知。她甚至还借用了我过去的一段经历加强了影响，还挺让人头疼。
t_en: Like March, I realized something was corrupting our cognition when I arrived at school. She even pulled from one of my past memories to strengthen the effect... It was pretty frustrating.

t_ch: 好在之前经历过类似的事情…这一次我倒是很快从认知污染中醒了过来。我干脆继续假扮老师，暗中把一部分学生藏了起来。
t_en: Luckily, I've dealt with something similar before... I snapped out of cognition corruption very fast this time, so I decided to keep playing the teacher and hide some students away in secret.

t_ch: 所以，如何解除火花造成的影响，你们有什么头绪吗？
t_en: So, any ideas on how to dispel Sparxie's influence?

t_ch: 你们将在「酒馆」得到的情报，以及行动计划告诉了三月和瓦尔特。
t_en: You told March and Welt about the intel you obtained at the "tavern" and your action plan.

t_ch: …也难怪这些学生们拆下了中庭的海浪景画。按照火花的要求，他们把景画拆成了三个部分：浪花、舢板和月亮。
t_en: ...No wonder these students took down the seascape painting from the central courtyard. Sparxie had them break it into three parts: the waves, the wooden boat, and the moon.

t_ch: 我不知道他们把浪花和月亮藏去了哪儿，但我把收进档案室里的「舢板」从学生手中收了回来。
t_en: I don't know where they hid the waves and the moon, but I managed to retrieve the "wooden boat" that was stored in the archive room from the students.

t_ch: 不愧是杨「老师」！
t_en: As expected from "Teacher" Yang!

t_ch: 景画中剩下的两个部分，我们也许已经有了线索——你们有没有见过一只…狸蕉？
t_en: We might have a lead on the other two painting pieces. Have you seen... a furbo-nana?

t_ch: 你说…狸猫？
t_en: You mean... a furbo?

t_ch: 我们派了一位本地记者潜进学校里，但很遗憾，他运气不太蕉，被火花抓了个现行。
t_en: We sent a local reporter into the school, but his luck turned sour-nana. Sparxie caught him red-handed.

t_ch: 记者被抓住前拍下了一张图书馆的照片，我想和{NICKNAME}去那儿看看。
t_en: Before he was taken away, he managed to take a photo of the library. I want to check it out with {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 那我和杨叔呢？既然有了方向，我们也不能呆在这儿啥也不干吧？
t_en: What about me and Mr. Yang? Now that we have a lead, no way we'll just sit here doing nothing!

t_ch: 小三月和我去搜寻狸猫记者的下落吧，也许还能探到剩下一件景画被藏去了哪儿。
t_en: March and I will go track down that furbo reporter. We might even find out where the last piece of the painting was hidden.

t_ch: 就这么办，一旦有什么线索，立刻告诉我们。
t_en: That's the plan. Let us know right away if you find any leads.

t_ch: 乖乖认输，旧型号！
t_en: Give it up, you outdated model!

t_ch: 闭嘴！吃我一锤！
t_en: Shush! Eat my hammer!

t_ch: 哎哟！可恶——！
t_en: Oww! Darn it!

t_ch: 他们会把景画藏在哪儿呢？
t_en: Where could they be hiding the landscape painting?

t_ch: 毕竟那是幻蕉之物，一旦被收进画轴之类东西里，要蕉起来可就有些麻烦了……
t_en: After all, it's an image-nana'ed thing. Once sealed in a scroll or something, it'll be a pain to retrieve, bana...

t_ch: 对了，看看记者最后留给我们的那张蕉片，也许能找到什么线索？
t_en: Oh right, let's check out that photo-nana the reporter left us. Maybe we can find some clues?

t_ch: 难蕉就在那个书架附近？
t_en: Could that be hidden near that bookshelf, bana?

t_ch: 到底藏在哪里呢？…怎么可能让你们知道嘛！
t_en: Where could it be...? Like I'd ever tell you!

t_ch: 火花大人亲口交代，要我们牢牢把守住这里。
t_en: Miss Sparxie herself ordered us to guard this place with our lives.

t_ch: 我说，你在一旁快给我们开个直播吧——
t_en: Hey, get that stream running...

t_ch: 现在的学生啊…不专心学业，只知道沉溺网络。
t_en: Kids these days... They ignore their studies just to focus on the internet.

t_ch: 作为绘世学院毕业的前辈，按规矩，我该给大家上一堂「课外补习」才行。
t_en: As an alum of Graphia Academy, it's my duty, as tradition dictates, to give you all a proper "extracurricular lesson."

t_ch: 学弟学妹们，准备好了吗？
t_en: Hey, juniors, ready to roll?

t_ch: 我们这是到哪儿了？
t_en: Where are we now?

t_ch: 这里和其他教室很相似，但似乎又有些不同……
t_en: This place looks just like any other classroom... but something's off.

t_ch: 四处找找吧。
t_en: Let's look around.

t_ch: 原来他们把「浪花」的部分藏在这里。
t_en: So this is where they stashed the "Wave."

t_ch: 你听见什么声音了吗，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: Did you hear something, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 有。
t_en: I did.

t_ch: 好像有谁在笑。
t_en: I think I heard someone laughing.

t_ch: 没有。
t_en: Nope.

t_ch: 我什么也没听见。
t_en: I didn't hear a thing.

t_ch: …不太对劲。这地方似乎藏着什么东西？
t_en: ...Something's not right. Is there something hidden here?

t_ch: 也不知谁将一册漫画杂志大剌剌地扔在桌上。
t_en: Someone carelessly tossed a comic magazine on the table.

t_ch: 「八四年7月<unbreak>20</unbreak>日，第<unbreak>29</unbreak>号刊，全新连载《超级蔬菜人》堂堂开启！」…真是好古早的漫画。
t_en: "July 20th, 1984, Issue #29, introducing the brand new series Super Leaf Walkers!"... Man, that's such an old-school comic.

t_ch: 课桌里留着一本笔记，有人将便签贴在了日历某页上。
t_en: There's a notebook in the desk, with a sticky note attached to a page of the calendar.

t_ch: 「校庆五十周年准备事宜…购买彩带、灯球…学生作品展筹备…<unbreak>84</unbreak>/<unbreak>07</unbreak>/<unbreak>16</unbreak>」
t_en: "<unbreak>50</unbreak>th Anniversary Preparations... Purchase streamers, lanterns... Student exhibition planning... <unbreak>84</unbreak>/<unbreak>07</unbreak>/<unbreak>16</unbreak>"

t_ch: 黑板一侧的粉笔痕迹光亮如新，像是被人当天写下。
t_en: Chalk marks gleam fresh on one side of the blackboard, as if written just today.

t_ch: 「乐园历<unbreak>1984</unbreak>年7月<unbreak>22</unbreak>日，值日生：美玲，满愿」
t_en: "Arcadian Calendar, July <unbreak>22</unbreak>nd <unbreak>1984</unbreak>. On duty: Belle, Fulwish"

t_ch: <unbreak>1984</unbreak>年7月<unbreak>22</unbreak>日？美玲？难道这间教室是……？
t_en: July <unbreak>22</unbreak>nd, <unbreak>1984</unbreak>? Belle? Could this classroom be...?

t_ch: 姬子，你没事吧？
t_en: Himeko, are you okay?

t_ch: 我们穿越了？
t_en: Did we just time travel?

t_ch: …前辈？你回来啦！
t_en: ...Senior? You're back!

t_ch: …美玲？
t_en: ...Belle?

t_ch: 真的是你啊，前辈！
t_en: It really is you, senior!

t_ch: 你的样子变了好多！我们差点就认不出你来了。可是，前辈那股飒爽的气质，怎样都掩盖不住啊。
t_en: You look so different! We almost didn't recognize you. Still, that dashing aura of yours simply can't be obscured.

t_ch: 我…还有其他许多许多的同学们，一直都在这里等着你啊。
t_en: I... and so many of us... we've been here waiting all this time.

t_ch: 真是抱歉，我离开了太久…真不像话，连一次都没有回来看望你们。
t_en: I'm so sorry... I was gone too long. It's unforgivable that I never came back to visit you all.

t_ch: …突然用一本正经的语气道歉，还真不像前辈会做的事情啊。
t_en: ...You, apologizing so seriously? That's not like you at all, senior.

t_ch: 是啊，难得重逢，至少应该笑一笑的。
t_en: Yeah... After all this time, the least I can do is smile.

t_ch: 可是，你们都已经不在人世了啊。
t_en: But... all of you are already dead.

t_ch: 姬子，这些学生究竟是……
t_en: Himeko... these students... Who are they?

t_ch: 他们是我的悔恨投下的影子…是一群因为意外埋葬在这里的年轻人。
t_en: They're the shadows cast by my past regrets... Young lives buried here by a tragic accident.

t_ch: …什么？
t_en: ...What?

t_ch: 不好，前辈。他来了…那家伙来了！
t_en: Oh no, senior. He's here... that thing is coming!

t_ch: …你说什么？
t_en: ...Wait, what did you just say?

t_ch: 教室的门合上了，不知何时，你已身在走廊。
t_en: The classroom door closed, and before you knew it, you were standing in the hallway.

t_ch: （从门缝中窥看）
t_en: (Peek through the door crack)

t_ch: 门缝仿佛被浓得化不开的黑暗填满，你什么也看不见。
t_en: The gap in the door seems filled with an impenetrable darkness, and you can't see a thing.

t_ch: （趴在门缝上听）
t_en: (Listen through the door crack)

t_ch: 你听见似是点点微雨轻轻洒落在玻璃窗上的声音。
t_en: You hear what sounds like light raindrops gently falling on the window.

t_ch: 教室内，有人小心踱着步子，踩踏过粘腻的地面…仿佛一位画家走在打落满地的油彩上。
t_en: In the classroom, someone carefully paced around, treading across the sticky floor... like a painter walking on spilled paint scattered all over the ground.

t_ch: （用力推门）
t_en: (Push the door with force)

t_ch: 教室的门纹丝不动，无论你用上多大的力气都无法推开它。
t_en: The classroom door won't budge an inch, no matter how much force you apply to it.

t_ch: …「浪花」入手了，我们该去和瓦尔特他们会合了。
t_en: ...We've got the "Wave" now. Time to regroup with Welt and the others.

t_ch: 可是这些学生…
t_en: But these students...

t_ch: 还有人被困在里面？
t_en: Aren't there still people trapped inside?

t_ch: 你无法解救他们，他们已是亡灵。袭击他们的不是火花，而是某个更为残忍的存在。
t_en: You can't save them. They're already ghosts. It wasn't Sparxie who attacked them, but something far more cruel.

t_ch: 他们遇害的时间，也并非我们可以改变的当下，而是…在十五年之前的某个黄昏。
t_en: And they... didn't die in the present, but during a certain twilight, fifteen years ago. We can't change their fates.

t_ch: 但，这绝对不是什么回到过去的时间旅行……
t_en: However, this definitely isn't some kind of time travel to the past...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>有人不知出于什么目的创造了这个空间…只为了在当事人面前，重演十五年前的幻月游戏中…一场发生在绘世学院里的血案。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Someone created this space for unknown reasons... only to re-enact before an eyewitness the bloodbath at Graphia Academy from the Phantasmoon Games fifteen years ago.</color>

t_ch: 十五年前的幻月游戏？
t_en: The Phantasmoon Games fifteen years ago?

t_ch: 你当时就在现场？
t_en: You were there at the time?

t_ch: 没错。不过，这和我们眼前要对付的敌人无关。
t_en: That's right. But that has nothing to do with the enemy we're facing right now.

t_ch: 我明白你现在一肚子疑问…但在解决火花后，才能有时间细说这段往事。
t_en: I know you have a lot of questions now... but we'll have time to talk about all this in detail after we deal with Sparxie.

t_ch: 走吧，该去和瓦尔特会合了，他发来了消息，他和三月找到了失踪的狸蕉记者——还有最后一块景画的下落。
t_en: Come on. It's time for us to meet up with Welt. He just messaged and said he and March found the missing furbo nana reporter, bana, and the final landscape painting piece.

t_ch: 你没事吧，狸猫先生。
t_en: You okay, Mr. Furbo?

t_ch: 幸亏咱们到的及时，不然你可要被当成靶子了啦。
t_en: Good thing we got here in time, or you would've ended up as a target practice dummy!

t_ch: 哔啵，本狸…差点就交代在这里了！
t_en: Beep boop, this furbo... nearly bit the dust!

t_ch: 社长大人你来啦！你一定会给本狸发慰问金的啵？
t_en: President, you're here! You're gonna give this furbo some hazard pay, right?

t_ch: 事情还没办成呢。
t_en: You didn't even finish the job.

t_ch: 所以，第三块景画呢？
t_en: So, where's the third landscape painting piece?

t_ch: 这位狸猫先生说，学生们把景画中的「月亮」放进了体育馆里。
t_en: This Mr. Furbo said the students put the "moon" inside the gymnasium.

t_ch: 到处都是月亮…
t_en: These moons... they're everywhere...

t_ch: 到处都是「球」…
t_en: These orbs... they're everywhere...

t_ch: 这…满地都是什么玩意儿？
t_en: What... in the world are all these things everywhere?

t_ch: 这是被称为「伊磨吉」的尘灵。
t_en: Wispae called "emotis."

t_ch: 火花一定是把月亮交给了其中某只伊磨吉保管了！
t_en: Sparxie must have given the moon to one of the emotis to look after!

t_ch: 这家伙还真是煞费苦心啊。一眼看过去，整个体育馆里到处都是「月亮」…这可怎么办！
t_en: She really went all out, didn't she? The whole gym's full of moons... Now what!?

t_ch: 论给人找麻烦的本事，无论花火还是火花，都那么令人头疼。
t_en: Be it Sparkle or Sparxie, both are equally just as headache-inducing.

t_ch: 大家一起抓住这些伊磨吉找找看吧！
t_en: Alright, everyone, let's round up these emotis and take a look!

t_ch: 你，老实交代，有没有藏起月亮！
t_en: You! Fess up! Did you stash the moon somewhere!?

t_ch: 被称为「伊磨吉」的尘灵发出一阵尖细的窃笑。
t_en: The wispae called "Emoti" lets out a sharp, shrill snicker.

t_ch: 我掰开它嘴看了看，这家伙应该没吞下「月亮」。
t_en: I pried open its mouth to take a look. Seems this little guy didn't swallow the moon.

t_ch: 这个球的质感…似乎与其他不同。
t_en: The texture of this ball... seems different from the others.

t_ch: 「伊磨吉」嘤嘤哭了起来。
t_en: "Emoti" bursts into soft, whiny sobs.

t_ch: 好了，别哭了，不会对你严刑拷打的。
t_en: Okay, okay, don't cry. No one's going to hurt you.

t_ch: 这只尘灵猛然撞向你，像一发出膛的炮弹！
t_en: This wispae suddenly charges against you like a cannon ball firing from its chamber

t_ch: 一只高高在上的伊磨吉，卡在了篮框里，如同踞于王座的君主，冷冷瞧着你们在底下忙碌。
t_en: A high-and-mighty Emoti is stuck fast in the basketball hoop. Perched there like a monarch upon a throne, it gazes down coldly at the frantic bustle of your efforts below.

t_ch: 你瞅啥？
t_en: You eyeballing me, emoti?

t_ch: 你有瞧见月亮藏在哪吗？
t_en: You know where the moon is hidden?

t_ch: 它的目光不自然地转向别处，嘴角却不经意露出得意的微笑，像是在说：没有膝盖的家伙，怎么配摘得天上的东西。
t_en: Its gaze shifts awkwardly away, but a smug grin sneaks onto its lips, as if thinking: "You've got no knees? Pfft, good luck reaching for the moon."

t_ch: 这个家伙笑得这么不怀好意，说不定它知道月亮被藏在哪里…但是，该怎么够着它呢？
t_en: That sneaky laugh... it probably does know where the moon is hiding... but how are we meant to even reach the thing?

t_ch: 这样一来，景画的第三部分「月亮」也到手了。
t_en: And with that, we've got our hands on the third part of the scenery, the "Moon".

t_ch: 好耶，也是时候该去和火花算一算这笔账了！
t_en: Alright! Time to settle the score with Sparxie!

t_ch: 我的球棒已经忍耐很久了！
t_en: My bat's been itching for some action!

t_ch: 火花既然敢将星穹列车作为目标，把我们当成幻月游戏中的踏蕉石，她就该有所准备，自己也会成为被收割的战利品。
t_en: Since Sparxie dared to target the Astral Express, treating us like stepping-nana stones in the Phantasmoon Games, she had better be ready to become a spoil of war herself.

t_ch: 接下来我和{NICKNAME}要前往中庭，重新拼合幻造景画，帮助花火对付火花。
t_en: {NICKNAME} and I will head to the atrium next, piece the imagenated landscape back together, and help Sparkle take down Sparxie.

t_ch: 瓦尔特、小三月，我想拜托你们去保护学校的学生——鸽川的浪花应该能压制他们所受的模因污染，但也许会让他们惊慌失措，闯进战斗的现场……
t_en: Welt, March, I'm counting on you to protect the students at the academy. Dovebrook's waves should suppress the memetic contamination they're experiencing, but they might panic and run into the combat area...

t_ch: …这一回，我不希望在幻月游戏中看到有无辜的人受伤。
t_en: ...This time, I truly hope no innocent people get hurt during the Phantasmoon Games.

t_ch: 放心吧，姬子姐，这些事就包在我和杨叔身上了。
t_en: Don't worry, Miss Himeko. Mr. Yang and I have got this.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你可要狠狠帮我教训一下那个把我们当成喜剧演员耍弄的小姑娘啊！
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you better give that girl a taste of her own medicine for messing with us like some comedy act!

t_ch: 出发！
t_en: Let's go!

t_ch: 看我的吧！
t_en: Just leave it to me!

t_ch: 放手，你扯到我头发了！
t_en: Let go! You're pulling my hair!

t_ch: 没错，扯的就是这里，给花火大人乖乖认输吧！
t_en: That's the whole point! Surrender to Miss Sparkle like a good girl!

t_ch: 把你的拳头从我脸上拿开！
t_en: Get your fist outta my face!

t_ch: 你先把脚给我挪开！
t_en: Then move your foot first!

t_ch: 花火和火花不见了？学生们也不见了？
t_en: Sparkle and Sparxie are gone? And the students too?

t_ch: 你们要是以为自己胜券在握…那可就太天真了！
t_en: If you think you've got this in the bag... you're in for a rude awakening!

t_ch: 「火花大会」还没开始呢！
t_en: SparxiCon hasn't even started!

t_ch: 你的「火花大会」该谢幕了！
t_en: Time for your SparxiCon grand finale!

t_ch: 因为我，星穹列车的无名客，将会以谒者的身份，向你发起挑战，粉碎你的「欢愉」！
t_en: Because I, the Nameless of the Astral Express, will challenge you as a fellow Supplicant, and crush your "Elation"!

t_ch: 向我挑战？——我愿意~♥
t_en: Challenge me? I'd love to fight you ♥

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，赶快把景画组合起来。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, hurry up and put the scenery pieces together.

t_ch: …你还好吗？{NICKNAME}？
t_en: ...Are you okay, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: …人都去哪儿了？
t_en: ...Where did everyone go?

t_ch: 看这边。
t_en: Look over here.

t_ch: 爻光将军？是幻觉吗？
t_en: General Yao Guang? Am I seeing things?

t_ch: 只是被「调律」连在一起罢了。
t_en: We're just "attuned" to each other, that's all.

t_ch: 感到目眩？不必担心，小小的副作用而已。
t_en: Feeling dizzy? Don't worry. It's just a minor side effect.

t_ch: 我第一次「开眼」时，有近一个月无法自理——放心吧，有我加护，片刻后便能恢复如常。
t_en: When I first "opened my eyes," I couldn't even conduct basic self-care for nearly a month. Don't worry, though. With my reinforcement, you'll be back to normal in a moment.

t_ch: 多谢将军。对了，你的头顶……
t_en: Thank you, general. By the way, above your head...

t_ch: 你能看到我头顶的签？
t_en: Can you see the sign above my head?

t_ch: 那么，<color=#dbc291ff>无论它是吉是凶…无论它会否变化，都请替我牢牢保管这个秘密，别让我知道，也别告诉其他任何人，好吗？</color>
t_en: So, <color=#dbc291ff>whether it brings weal or woe... whether it changes or not, please keep this secret safe for me. Don't let me know, and don't let anyone else, okay?</color>

t_ch: 那得来点好处吧？
t_en: What's in it for me?

t_ch: 喂，我可是特地来帮了你的忙。就不能给我打个一折，当是结个善缘吗？
t_en: Hey, I went out of my way to help you here. Can't you give me a discount? Consider it good karma!

t_ch: 星期日看不到你的签吗？
t_en: Can't Sunday see your sign?

t_ch: 很遗憾，他看不见。作为观测者，你的目光比他的更深远……
t_en: I'm afraid not. As an observer, your gaze reaches far deeper than his...

t_ch: 你在害怕什么？
t_en: What are you afraid of?

t_ch: 在刚才那一瞬间，{RUBY_B#星核}你{RUBY_E#}的眼洞见了我的命运……
t_en: In that fleeting moment, {RUBY_B#Stellaron's}your{RUBY_E#} eyes gained insight into my destiny...

t_ch: 对妄想颠覆自身命数的人来说，最好的方法就是不要窥视命运太久，尤其不要让{RUBY_B#你和我}强大的观测者{RUBY_E#}这么做。
t_en: For those who wish to defy their own destiny, the best approach is to avoid gazing too long into fate, especially not letting {RUBY_B#you and I}powerful observers{RUBY_E#} do so.

t_ch: 知道了理由，想必你一定愿意帮我这个小忙，保守这个秘密？
t_en: Now that you know the reason, surely you'll do me this small favor and keep this secret?

t_ch: 我会保守秘密的。
t_en: I will keep this a secret.

t_ch: 谢谢。当然啦，也要留神可别说漏嘴了，不然……
t_en: Thanks. And of course, be careful not to let it slip, or else...

t_ch: 可我要是说漏嘴了呢？
t_en: But what if I let it slip?

t_ch: 哼哼，你要是敢泄密的话……
t_en: Heh heh, if you dare to spill the secret...

t_ch: 我就要满世界跟人到处乱说！你的真实身份…是一位「前星核猎手」，没错吧？
t_en: I'll tell everyone all about your true identity! ...You're a "former Stellaron Hunter," aren't you?

t_ch: 那样一来，会产生怎样的连锁反应呢？我简直不敢想！
t_en: Oh, just imagine the chain reaction that would cause! I don't even want to think about it!

t_ch: 不觉得这很像一幕喜剧的开场吗？两个明明相识不久的人，却要互相保守对方命运的秘密——啧，咱们这也算是「过命」的交情了？
t_en: Doesn't this feel like the opening scene of a comedy? Two people who barely know each other, yet they're keeping each other's destiny a secret. Tsk, I guess you could say we're now "partners in fate," huh?

t_ch: 也不奇怪，毕竟「喜剧」和「卜卦」，最不能缺少的都是<color=#dbc291ff>意外与阴差阳错</color>，对吧？
t_en: No surprise there. After all, both "comedy" and "fortune-telling" thrive on <color=#dbc291ff>unexpected twists and turns of fate</color>, don't they?

t_ch: 好了，看来副作用好的差不多了，咱们也该道别了。
t_en: Alright, looks like the side effects have worn off. Time for us to say goodbye.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你没事吧？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, are you okay?

t_ch: …刚才，到底发生了什么？
t_en: ...What just happened?

t_ch: 我稀里糊涂的，完全没看清。只记得火花和无名客打了起来。
t_en: I was so confused. I couldn't see a thing. All I remember is Sparxie and the Nameless got into a fight.

t_ch: 我看清了！列车的无名客一棒子把火花拍到了墙上！何等惊人的力量！
t_en: I saw everything! The Nameless from the Express smashed Sparxie right into the wall with one swing! That strength was unreal!

t_ch: 好、好炫酷的战斗…不愧是银河里来的大英雄，真是酷毙了！
t_en: Th–That was such an epic fight... As expected from a cosmic hero, that was seriously awesome!

t_ch: 星穹列车！星穹列车！星穹列车！
t_en: Astral Express! Astral Express! Astral Express! Astral Express!

t_ch: 看起来，学生们都从污染中恢复了。
t_en: Looks like the students have all recovered from the contamination.

t_ch: …他们真的恢复了吗？
t_en: ...Did they recover?

t_ch: 哇，我一直以为「击败反物质军团的英雄」只是吹牛呢。
t_en: Wow, I always thought the whole "hero who defeated the Antimatter Legion" thing was just hot air.

t_ch: 那一击，让人有点害怕啊……
t_en: That hit... gave me chills.

t_ch: 害怕？不该羡慕吗？我好想成为那样的强者啊。
t_en: Chills? You should be impressed! Wow, I wish I could be that strong.

t_ch: 你听说了吗？这个打败火花的{F#女生}{M#男生}，就是《苍天航路绒绒号》的主角原型…那个浣熊！
t_en: Did you hear? The {M#guy}{F#girl} who beat Sparxie is the original inspiration for the protagonist in Fluffy Across the Blue. You know... that raccoon!

t_ch: {F#她}{M#他}好{F#飒}{M#帅}啊！
t_en: {M#He's crazy cool!}{F#She's such a badass!}

t_ch: 火花完全被比下去了啊。
t_en: Sparxie was totally outshone!

t_ch: 被她吸收的瞬间…我还以为一切都完蛋了呢。
t_en: When she absorbed me... I thought it was all over.

t_ch: 还好，小灰毛，是你赢了。「开拓」得一分！
t_en: Thankfully, Gray Hair, you won this one. One point for the Trailblaze!

t_ch: 你真的是面具吗？
t_en: Are you really just a mask?

t_ch: 这谁知道啊！但你不觉得这个说法真是酷到没朋友吗？
t_en: Who knows! But come on, doesn't that sound insanely cool?

t_ch: 我要把这个说法放进《花火大人的一百三十二个起源故事集》里去！
t_en: I'm totally adding this to Miss Sparkle's 132 Origin Stories!

t_ch: 也许我们每个人都是某人蜕下的一张面具也说不定呢！
t_en: Maybe we're all just masks that someone else has shed!

t_ch: 「酒馆」不会派人追杀我吧？
t_en: The tavern won't send assassins after me, right?

t_ch: 「酒馆」？哈哈，他们也许会为你打败火花的事儿热热闹闹吵上几天。也许还会有不开眼的愚者要来挑战你……
t_en: The tavern? Haha, they might kick up a fuss about Sparxie's defeat for a few days. Maybe some foolish hotheads might even come knocking to fight you...

t_ch: 但以我对他们的了解，三天一过，「酒馆」就会把这件事抛到脑后，像从来没发生过一样。
t_en: But trust me, give it a couple days and the tavern will act like none of it ever happened.

t_ch: 接下来你打算怎么办？
t_en: So, what's your plan now?

t_ch: 如果我真的是面具的话，当然应该回到佩戴者的脸上啊。
t_en: If I really am a mask, then naturally I should return to my wearer's face.

t_ch: 但如果我不是…那我也应该收回自己丢失的面具，不是吗？
t_en: But if I'm not... then maybe I need to reclaim the mask I've lost.

t_ch: 我明白了。
t_en: I understand.

t_ch: 火花恐怕也没想到，「幻月游戏」的第一场奇迹并非「人人都会成为她」…而是「列车英雄击败了散播病毒的反派」。
t_en: Sparxie probably didn't expect that the first miracle of the Phantasmoon Games wouldn't be "Everyone becomes her," but "The Express hero defeats the villain spreading the virus."

t_ch: 比起成为投射人们渴望的网络主播，显然星穹列车无畏的开拓者更吸引人眼球。
t_en: Compared to a streamer reflecting people's desires, the fearless Trailblazer of the Astral Express is more of an attention-grabber.

t_ch: 这就是幻月游戏？
t_en: So this is the Phantasmoon Games, huh?

t_ch: 不错。谒者们有时合作，有时彼此攻伐…就像硬币，不落地谁也不知道它会露出哪一面来。
t_en: That's right. Sometimes the Supplicants work together, sometimes they clash... It's like a coin toss. No telling which side will land until the moment it hits the ground

t_ch: 至少这一次，我希望，它不要以血流成河的方式收场。
t_en: At least this time, I hope it doesn't end in bloodshed.

t_ch: 我赢了，有什么好处？
t_en: What do I get if I win?

t_ch: 在谒见阿哈的路上，你比别人更进一步。此时此刻，二相乐园的{RUBY_B#愿力}关注{RUBY_E#}正不断涌向你…和你佩戴的面具。
t_en: You're one step closer to an audience with Aha. Right now, all of Planarcadia is {RUBY_B#Wishpower}watching{RUBY_E#} you, and all of their attention's flooding over you... and the mask you wear.

t_ch: 好好珍惜这份关注吧。从此刻开始，你将代表「开拓」，向欢愉的世界展现「何为欢愉」。
t_en: Make the most of that attention. From now on, you'll represent the Trailblaze to show this world of Elation "what Elation is."

t_ch: 该怎么处置火花呢？
t_en: What should we do with Sparxie?

t_ch: 展现你的仁慈，或者残忍…不不，我只是开个玩笑，把球棒放低。
t_en: Show mercy, or don't... No, no, I'm just kidding. Lower your bat.

t_ch: 对游戏的败者，乐园的观众们可不仁慈。她很快就会失去愿力，「火花病毒」也会失去活性。
t_en: The audience in this arcadia doesn't go easy on losers at the games. She'll lose her Wishpower soon enough, and the "Sparxie Virus" will fizzle out.

t_ch: 你可以把她交给异常防御部囚禁，又或者放她自由——作为谒者，身为所有人目光的焦点，我想你肯定已经做好决定了，对吧？
t_en: You can hand her over to the Department of Aberration Defense for imprisonment, or set her free. As a Supplicant who's become the focus of all, I'm sure you've already made your decision, right?

t_ch: 胜利者，来行使你的权力了吗？好吧，火花花对此并无意见。
t_en: So, victor, here to claim your prize? Very well, Sparxicle has no objections to that.

t_ch: 那么，你想怎么处置我呢？把我交给异常防御部，当成囚犯看管起来？以我对你的了解，你应该没那么绝情吧。
t_en: So what'll it be? Gonna hand me off to the Department of Aberration Defense, lock me up as a hardened criminal? But knowing you... you're not that heartless, are you?

t_ch: 又或者，你想夺走火花花的直播账号作为惩罚？我可以二十四小时直播赞美你的勇武~♥
t_en: Or maybe you want to take away my streaming account as punishment? I'll livestream <unbreak>24</unbreak>/7 and sing praises of your valor! ♥

t_ch: 把你的面具给我。
t_en: Hand me your mask.

t_ch: 好啦，好啦~就让绘世家的归绘世家……
t_en: Alright, alright... Let what belongs to the Graphia family return to the Graphia family...

t_ch: 可别被眼前的胜利冲昏了头脑~距离觐见乐子神，你还有很远很远的路要走。
t_en: But don't let this little win get to your head! You've still got a long, long way to go before you meet The Laughter.

t_ch: 我们可以继续合作。在幻月游戏中结交一位朋友，总好过多一个敌人？
t_en: We can keep working together. Better to have a friend at the Phantasmoon Games than another enemy, right?

t_ch: …然后呢？你就没什么想说的了？
t_en: ...What, no parting words?

t_ch: 我要把你交给花火处置。
t_en: I'm letting Sparkle decide your fate.

t_ch: 哼。
t_en: Hmph.

t_ch: 喜欢这个结局吗，火花花？
t_en: How do you like this ending, Sparxicle?

t_ch: 旧型号…你和{RUBY_B#开拓者}你抱的那条大腿{RUBY_E#}赢了，我无话可说。
t_en: Old model... you and {RUBY_B#Trailblazer}your powerful supporter{RUBY_E#} won this one. I've got no comeback.

t_ch: 作为面具，打算怎么处置你的本体呢？
t_en: As a mask, what do you plan to do with your original self?

t_ch: 谁当面具，谁是本体…不是该由站着的那个来决定吗？你的计划完蛋了，你的「欢愉」被大家淘汰出局了，你才该叫「旧型号」。
t_en: Who's the mask and who's the original... Shouldn't that be decided by the one left standing? Your plan is finished. Your Elation has been eliminated. You're the one who should be called the "old model".

t_ch: 这可不是我的「欢愉」，这是大家选择的「欢愉」。更何况——
t_en: That Elation isn't mine. It's the Elation everyone chose. And besides...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>一开始想出这个主意的家伙，不是你吗？</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>Wasn't it your idea in the first place?</color>

t_ch: 你说啥？！
t_en: WHAT!?

t_ch: 是你让我把你抛下，也是你让我带上这个点子还有相关的记忆远走高飞，自己去实现梦想的啊。
t_en: You told me to leave you behind. You also told me to take the idea and its relevant memories, and go chase my dream.

t_ch: 啊这这这这……
t_en: Uhhhh...

t_ch: <size=36>这确实像是花火大人会做的事啊！</size>
t_en: <size=36>That's totally something Miss Sparkle would do!</size>

t_ch: 喜欢这个结局吗，花火？
t_en: How do you like this ending, Sparkle?

t_ch: 那句笑话怎么说来着？「假面愚者要是没了假面，也就只剩下一个傻瓜了」。
t_en: How does that joke go again? "Take the Mask from a Masked Fool, and what's left but a fool."

t_ch: 「但假面愚者要是没了愚者，剩下的也不过是一张面具」。
t_en: "But take the Fool out of a Masked Fool, and all that's left is the mask."

t_ch: 来吧，花火~♥一场闹剧该结束了，谢幕是必不可少的礼仪哟。咱们俩也该合二为一了吧？
t_en: Come on, Sparkle~♥ Time to end this little charade. No show is complete without a curtain call. Shall we become one at last?

t_ch: Bang！
t_en: Bang!

t_ch: 观众们，你们玩的开心吗？
t_en: Ladies and gentlemen, are you having fun?

t_ch: 来自花火大人的友情提示：…别小看幻月游戏啊，像「火花大会」这样美好的揭幕仪式，可不会再有了喔？
t_en: A friendly reminder from Miss Sparkle: ...don't underestimate the Phantasmoon Games! You won't get another opening act as spectacular as this "SparxiCon," you know?

t_ch: 玩具枪响后，花火与火花已不在原地。
t_en: After the toy gun went off, Sparkle and Sparxie were no longer there.

t_ch: 火花…呃不，花火？她们俩到底谁是谁？
t_en: Sparxie... uh no, Sparkle? Which one is which?

t_ch: 也许，只是一位假面愚者想在神明的注视下玩弄一次无伤大雅的诡计。
t_en: Perhaps it was just a Masked Fool playing a harmless trick under the watchful eyes of the gods.

t_ch: 又也许，她是想提醒我们…二相乐园的「欢愉」，已经变成了某种神明都认不出来的东西了。
t_en: Or maybe, she just wanted to remind us... that the Elation in Planarcadia has become something even the gods no longer recognize.

t_ch: 无论如何，这里的事情都收场了。{NICKNAME}，我们该去拆穿另一桩诡计了。
t_en: In any case, everything here has come to an end. {NICKNAME}, we should move on and unravel the next plot.

t_ch: 什么？
t_en: What?

t_ch: 我们去到的那个「时间停滞在十五年前的教室」。
t_en: Let's go to that classroom, where "time froze fifteen years ago."

t_ch: …那间诡异的教室？
t_en: ...That creepy classroom?

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: Indeed.
